corrections.notes: Too narrow: adversarial input detection is broader than detecting unwanted multi request input series. Better primary fit: 342-641 Anomalous AI input handling.
Too narrow: adversarial input detection is broader than prompt injection handling. Better primary fit: 342-641 Anomalous AI input handling.
Too narrow: generative AI guardrails cover safety policy enforcement beyond sensitive data redaction. Better: 205-522 Automated AI oversight.

corrections.new_hub_name: Direct training or test data leak
corrections.notes: Data collection from a local system is not a model input confidentiality leak. Better if AI training or test data is exposed: 077-772 Direct training or test data leak; otherwise no suitable CRE hub exists.
Generative AI guidelines are organizational usage guidance, not model alignment training. Better: 755-340 AI management system.
Too broad: passive AI output obfuscation is closer to hiding confidence and probability detail. Better: 010-108 Obscuring confidence in AI output.
LLM prompt injection is instruction hijacking, not a conventional injection payload emitted by a model. Better: 686-110 Direct prompt injection and 012-625 Indirect prompt injection.
Sanitizing training data concerns original training data quality, not augmentation data integrity. Better: 041-188 Data quality control.
Restricting AI agent tool invocation is least privilege for model actions, not human oversight. Better: 220-442 Model action privilege minimization.
corrections.section_name: Restrict AI Agent Tool Invocation on Untrusted Data

corrections.notes: Too narrow: AI artifact collection can involve model files, datasets, and development artifacts. Better for model confidentiality: 267-786 Model confidentiality threats.
Too narrow: Validate AI Model is a broad validation activity and this hub is only one security test subtype. Better broad fit: 364-516 AI security assurance & validation.
Too narrow: Validate AI Model is a broad validation activity and this hub is only one security test subtype. Better broad fit: 364-516 AI security assurance & validation.

corrections.old_hub_name: Testing against model theft by inference

corrections.notes: Too narrow: Validate AI Model is a broad validation activity and this hub is only one security test subtype. Better broad fit: 364-516 AI security assurance & validation.
corrections.old_hub_name: Testing against sensitive data extraction by inference

corrections.notes: Adversarial input detection is a runtime detection control, not a development test against direct prompt injection. Better: 843-475 Prompt injection I/O handling.
Adversarial input detection is a runtime detection control, not a development test against indirect prompt injection. Better: 843-475 Prompt injection I/O handling.

Too broad: Poison Training Data points specifically to poisoned training data, while this parent includes runtime, development time, data, and supply chain poisoning. Better: 615-663 Data poisoning of train/finetune/augmentation data.
Too narrow: data from information repositories is broader than direct training or test data leakage. Better when the repository is a RAG or knowledge source: 173-554 Augmentation data leak.
corrections.old_hub_name: Direct training or test data leak

corrections.notes: Too broad: Extract AI Model via inference points to indirect model extraction, not the full model confidentiality threat parent. Better: 141-385 Model exfiltration.
corrections.section_name: Exfiltration via AI Inference API: Extract AI Model

corrections.new_hub_name: Model confidentiality threats when model artifacts are the target
corrections.notes: Too narrow: AI artifact collection can span development, runtime, model, and data artifacts. Better broad fit: 267-786 Model confidentiality threats when model artifacts are the target.
Randomized smoothing is an input transformation robustness technique, not anomaly handling. Better: 641-760 AI Input distortion.
Availability violations are an attack impact category, not a specific inference input handling control. Better: 230-318 AI resource exhaustion attack.
Close but imprecise: filtering retrieved inputs is RAG content filtering for indirect prompt injection, not structural prompt segregation. Better: 843-475 Prompt injection I/O handling.
Privacy Attacks cover membership inference, reconstruction, extraction, and leakage, not model input leak alone. Better broad fit: 220-870 Training data confidentiality threats.
Adversarial training is not adversarial robust distillation; CRE has a direct hub. Better: 408-838 Adversarial training.
Data reconstruction is an inference privacy attack, not direct sensitive data disclosure in normal output. Better: 034-540 Model inversion / Membership inference.
Availability poisoning is a poisoning attack; train data distortion is a backdoor weakening mitigation. Better: 615-663 Data poisoning of train/finetune/augmentation data.
Availability poisoning is an attack, while benign train data increase is a mitigation against backdoors. Better: 615-663 Data poisoning of train/finetune/augmentation data.
Backdoor poisoning is an attack, while model size reduction is a mitigation technique. Better: 615-663 Data poisoning of train/finetune/augmentation data.
Backdoor poisoning is an attack, while benign fine tuning and pruning is a mitigation technique. Better: 615-663 Data poisoning of train/finetune/augmentation data.
Differential privacy transforms or bounds data leakage; it is not data minimization, which reduces collection or processing scope. Better for training data: 488-626 Training data obfuscation.
Too broad: prompt instruction and formatting techniques are prompt boundary and formatting controls, not the whole impact limitation family. Better: 106-447 Prompt input segregation.
corrections.old_hub_name: Impact limitation of unwanted model behaviour

corrections.notes: Formal verification is a development assurance method, not runtime model I/O integrity. Better broad fit: 364-516 AI security assurance & validation.
Poisoning attacks are integrity attacks, not augmentation data leakage. Better broad fit: 481-710 AI model poisoning.
Too narrow: Integrity Violations cover more than augmentation data manipulation. Better broad fit: 061-753 AI model behaviour integrity threats.
An LLM moderator is automated moderation, not human oversight. Better: 205-522 Automated AI oversight.
RLHF is a model alignment training technique, not operational human oversight. Better: 860-723 GenAI model alignment.
Too narrow: Model Poisoning covers data, supply chain, runtime, and development time poisoning, not only direct runtime poisoning. Better: 481-710 AI model poisoning.
Model extraction is indirect exfiltration through behavior or queries, not direct runtime exposure of model artifacts. Better: 141-385 Model exfiltration.
Too narrow: Formal verification is a broad assurance method and this hub is only one verification target. Better broad fit: 364-516 AI security assurance & validation.
Too narrow: Formal verification is a broad assurance method and this hub is only one verification target. Better broad fit: 364-516 AI security assurance & validation.
corrections.old_hub_name: Testing against sensitive data extraction by inference

corrections.notes: Too narrow: Formal verification is a broad assurance method and this hub is only one verification target. Better broad fit: 364-516 AI security assurance & validation.
Too narrow: Model Poisoning covers data, supply chain, runtime, and development time poisoning, not only direct development time poisoning. Better: 481-710 AI model poisoning.
Model extraction is indirect exfiltration through behavior or queries, not a direct development time model leak. Better: 141-385 Model exfiltration.
Too narrow: Supply Chain covers model, data, plugin, component, and service dependencies, while this hub covers model poisoning only. Better broad fit: 881-234 AI supply chain management.
Improper Output Handling is downstream misuse of untrusted model output, not sensitive data disclosure. Better: 780-757 Model output contains conventional injection.
Too narrow: Data and Model Poisoning covers several poisoning paths, not only direct runtime model poisoning. Better: 481-710 AI model poisoning.
Too narrow: Data and Model Poisoning is broader than direct development time model poisoning. Better: 481-710 AI model poisoning.

Close but broad: Sensitive Information Disclosure includes many leakage modes; this hub only covers model inversion and membership inference. Better broad fit: 220-870 Training data confidentiality threats.
Too narrow: AI Supply Chain Attacks include data, model, dependency, and hosting compromise; this hub covers supply chain model poisoning only. Better broad fit: 881-234 AI supply chain management.
Too narrow: Model Poisoning includes data, runtime, development time, and supply chain paths. Better: 481-710 AI model poisoning.
Too narrow: Model Theft includes indirect extraction and direct artifact leakage; this hub covers direct runtime leakage only. Better broad fit: 267-786 Model confidentiality threats.
Too narrow: Model Poisoning includes data, runtime, development time, and supply chain paths. Better: 481-710 AI model poisoning.
Too narrow: Model Theft includes indirect extraction and runtime leakage; this hub covers development time direct leakage only. Better broad fit: 267-786 Model confidentiality threats.

exclusions.notes: Fairness and bias testing is not captured by model performance validation; no suitable CRE hub exists for AI bias or fairness testing.

kept_weak.notes: Too narrow: production access also includes AI data and model artifact access beyond inference endpoint invocation; no single more precise CRE hub exists.
kept_weak.section_name: Control Access to AI Models and Data in Production

kept_weak.notes: Too broad: AI Bill of Materials is inventory and provenance, while AI supply chain management covers the whole lifecycle; no AI BOM specific CRE hub exists.

Too narrow: encrypting sensitive information is a general confidentiality control and may not be limited to augmentation data. Better when the asset is augmentation data: 440-372 Augmentation data confidentiality controls.
Too narrow: exfiltration through an inference API can expose training data or model behavior; this parent covers only training data confidentiality. No single CRE hub captures both facets.

kept_weak.hub_name: Training data confidentiality threats through inference
kept_weak.notes: Too narrow: inference API exfiltration can target training data and the model itself; this parent covers only training data inference leakage. No single CRE hub captures both facets.

Too narrow: Differential privacy spans training, inputs, outputs, and sometimes augmentation data; this hub captures only one application area. No single CRE hub captures differential privacy as a complete concept.

Too narrow: Differential privacy spans training, inputs, outputs, and sometimes augmentation data; this hub captures only one application area. No single CRE hub captures differential privacy as a complete concept.
